name: AvbSetProperty
title: AvbSetProperty
syntax: dword AvbSetProperty(char propertyName[], qword value); // form 2
dword AvbSetProperty(dword objHandle, char propertyName[], long value); // form 3
dword AvbSetProperty(dword objHandle, char propertyName[], dword length, byte value[]); // form 4
dword AvbSetProperty(dword objHandle, char propertyName[], qword value); // form 5
description: The behavior of the AVB IL can be configured using properties.

Properties can be set for the current bus context (form 1-2) as well as on an object-specific basis for talkers (form 3-4). The default settings for the properties can be obtained from the table below.

parameters.name: StreamReservation
parameters.description: Stream Reservation is not supported. Set this property to 0.

parameters.name: VlanId
parameters.description: The VLAN ID to be used for sending or receiving AVB packets.

parameters.name: RtpPort
parameters.description: Local client port used for RTP via UDP. RTP is always used in conjunction with RTCP. The RTCP UDP port number is one higher than the RTP port number.

parameters.name: RtspAddress
parameters.description: Remote server address used for RT(S)P via TCP. If not set, no connection attempt to an RTSP server is made when AvbListen is called.

parameters.name: FramesPerPacket
parameters.type: Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible).
parameters.description: Constant number of frames per AVTPDU.

parameters.name: VlanPriority
parameters.description: The VLAN Priority to be used for sending AVB packets.

parameters.name: PresentationTimeOffset
parameters.type: Deduced from SR class and frame rate (if possible)
parameters.description: Presentation Time Offset in [ns]. The Presentation Time Offset is the time difference between the Presentation Time and the time the sample is handed over to the MAC's egress buffer as part of an AVB packet that is automatically filled with the corresponding Presentation Timestamp. This time must account for the Max Transit Time inherent to the SR class of the network as illustrated in IEEE 1722-2011, Figure 6 - AVTP Timing Reference Planes.

parameters.name: RtpPayloadType
parameters.description: The RTP Payload Type describes the media encoding of the RTP stream's payload samples. This parameter is automatically set according to the first entry defined in the mapping table in the CANoeOptions dialog for a specific media type. If a distinct type should be used set this property on the talker.

parameters.name: objHandle
parameters.description: Handle of a talker or listener.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Length of the value buffer passed.

availability: 8.2 SP2: form 1, 3 10.0: form 2, 4, property VlanId 10.0 SP3: property RtspAddress 12.0 SP4: form 5, property PresentationTimeOffset 14: property RtpPayloadType
2.0 SP2: form 1, 3 2.2: form 3, 4, property VlanId 2.2 SP3: property RtspAddress 5.0: form 5, property PresentationTimeOffset 5.0 SP3: property RtpPayloadType
